title: BharatSearch
subtitle: A Vernacular-First Discovery Engine for the Next Billion Users
problem_statement: In the Indian market, traditional search engines remain gatekept by English-centric design and rigid keyword-matching logic. Over 400 million users in Tier 2 and Tier 3 cities face 'input anxiety' when forced to type in Romanized scripts or navigate complex text-heavy results. There is a fundamental gap between user intent expressed in local dialects and the algorithm's ability to provide hyper-local, verified, and actionable information.

pillars.title: The Input Anxiety Barrier
pillars.insight: Users often abandon searches when they cannot find the right English word, leading to a reliance on 'trial and error' typing that creates deep frustration.

pillars.title: Validation through Voice
pillars.insight: Indian users exhibit higher trust in information that is communicated via audio or video compared to static text blocks which feel 'official' but 'unrelatable'.

pillars.title: Peer-Led Authority
pillars.insight: Search intent is often social; users look for what their local community trusts rather than what an algorithm ranks highest globally.

artifacts.label: Strategy
artifacts.title: Defining the Challenges
questions: How might we make digital information accessible to users who prefer voice over typing?
How might we bridge the gap between regional dialects and global search indexing?
How might we build a trust-based search ecosystem for first-time internet users?

personas.name: Rajesh Kumar
personas.quote: I know what I want to find, but I don't know how to spell it in English.
personas.bio: Rajesh has transitioned to a smartphone recently. He uses WhatsApp for business but finds Google intimidating because the results often feel irrelevant to his local supply chain needs.

points.action: Replace the standard keyboard-first interface with a giant, central pulsating microphone button.
points.benefit: Users feel invited to speak naturally instead of struggling to type correctly.

points.action: Merge search results with a 'Verified by Locals' community badge and chat feature.
points.benefit: Builds immediate trust and allows users to ask follow-up questions to real people.

points.action: Use the camera to translate shop signs or product labels in real-time within the search flow.
points.benefit: Helps users understand the physical world around them through their phone screen.

points.action: Change the layout from list-view to large, color-coded visual cards with big icons.
points.benefit: Makes it much easier to scan and find information without needing to read every word.

pivot.before: A keyword-based, English-heavy global index that rewards SEO optimization over local relevance.
pivot.pivot_logic: Shifting the core interaction from 'Query-Response' to 'Conversation-Discovery' using LLM-based vernacular processing.
pivot.after: A voice-first, multi-modal hub that treats regional dialects as primary citizens and prioritizes local, verified utility.

stages.actions: User feels the need to find a local service.
stages.friction: Unsure if the app understands their specific dialect.

stages.actions: User taps the voice icon to speak a query.
stages.friction: Background noise interference during voice capture.
Differentiating between paid ads and organic local results.

stages.actions: User calls the business directly from results.
stages.friction: Broken links or outdated contact numbers.

content.prompt: What are you looking for today?

annotations.title: Dynamic Dialect Detection
annotations.rationale: Automatically switches the UI language based on the spoken audio input to reduce manual configuration.

annotations.title: Visual-Cue Results
annotations.rationale: Every search result includes a prominent image or video thumbnail to provide context before the user clicks.

annotations.title: Trust Badging
annotations.rationale: Uses a 'Green Check' system for businesses verified by physical local agents to prevent fraud.
